On 27 April, a measles outbreak was reported in Blue Nile state in southeastern Sudan, with Ihskap and Madit localities in Kurmuk district most affected. The first suspected case was reported on 15 March. Since early April, at least 1,000 cases have been reported, with 233 deaths. Children between 5-10 years old are most affected. Kurmuk district, located in southern Blue Nile, is an area where non-state armed groups are active and have levels of control over territory in the area.

In Yobe state in Northeast Nigeria, over 100 cases of Diphtheria have been reported, with at least 15 deaths, since early April. Over 60% of reported cases are in women, and nearly 50% in children aged 5-19.

As the conflict in Sudan continues, more than 700,000 people have fled Sudan. At least 230,000 refugees from Sudan have arrived in Chad . More refugees are likely to arrive as the situation in Sudan continues.

Over 600,000 people in Guatemala are facing IPC4 levels of food insecurity, in part due to drought caused by El Niño. At least 20 of the country's 22 departments are reporting a loss of crops due to drought, disproportionately affecting indigenous communities. At least 6 deaths of children under 5 due to food insecurity have been reported, with dozens more suspected.

Over 350,000 people in Honduras are facing IPC4 levels of food insecurity, in part due to drought caused by El Niño and particularly impacting rural areas. Conditions are expected to worsen as El Niño and the drought persists, resulting in further food insecurity, crop loss, and cattle death.

As the conflict in Sudan continues, at least 168,000 refugees have fled to South Sudan, with more expected to arrive as the situation progresses. In addition, over 350,000 people in Unity and Northern Bahr El Ghazal states, where refugees from Sudan are arriving, are facing IPC4 levels of food insecurity, including over 7,000 in IPC5.  Please note we are classifying the situation in Sudan and refugee movement to other countries separately.

Since May 2023, clashes in Plateau State in north-central Nigeria have resulted in at least 37,000 displacements and 215 deaths. The clashes and attacks have been concentrated in Mangu Local Government Area in Plateau State.

On 0 6 June, the Nova Kakhovka dam in the Kherson region of southern Ukraine was destroyed, flooding the surrounding areas. The floods have displaced at least 1,375 people while over 1 million people have been affected and are facing water shortages. Over 25,000 people in the Russian-controlled left bank of the Dnipro River cannot access humanitarian services.

On 23 July, flash floods destroyed dozens of homes, affecting over 1,200 people and killing at least 30. The flash floods affected four provinces in central Afghanistan - Kabul, Wardak, Ghazni, and Parwan.

On 26 July, Typhoon Doksuri made landfall in the northern Philippines, affecting over 500,000 people and displacing at least 42,000. The typhoon has resulted in at least 13 deaths, with dozens more missing, and has caused damange to agriculture and infrstructure.

Since July 17, heavy rains and flash floods have resulted in the deaths of 191 people and displaced over 1,400. The flooding has affected four provinces, including Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Sindh, Balochistan, and Punjab.

Since June 14, over 9,800 people fleeing heavy fighting in Me Se, Bawlakhe, and Shadaw Townships in eastern Myanmar have arrived to  Mae Hong Son Province in northwestern Thailand.  Many of those displaced to Thailand had already been internally displaced in Myanmar, living in Daw Noe Khu IDP settlement in Shadaw Township.

Since May, heavy rains and flooding have affected over 50,000 people and displaced at least 2,000 in Northwest Ecuador. Severe rains are expected to continue due to the impacts of El Nino, particularly affecting the provinces of Manabi, Los Rios, Guayas, and Esmeraldas.

Since 5 August, over 300,000 people have been affected  by severe rain and flash flooding in the Chittagong division in the south east part of Bangladesh. Cox's Bazaar has been particularly impacted, with over 15,000 affected and 1,500 displaced within the settlement.

Since 25 July, heavy rains and flash floods across Pakistan have resulted in the deaths of 205 people and displaced over 11,000. The flooding has affected all four provinces of the country, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Sindh, Balochistan, and Punjab.

Over 600,000 people in Guatemala are facing IPC4 levels of food insecurity, in part due to the impacts of El Nino At least 20 of the country's 22 departments reported a loss of crops due to drought, which disproportionately affected indigenous communities. Subsequent heavy rains have affected remaining crops, particularly in the western part of the country since the start of August.

Over 350,000 people in Honduras are facing IPC 4 levels of food insecurity, in part due to drought caused by El Niño, which is particularly impacting rural areas. Conditions are expected to worsen as El Niño and the drought persist, resulting in further food insecurity, crop loss, and cattle death.

Over 120,000 ethnic Armenians in Nagorno-Karabakh, a region contested by Azerbaijan and the Armenian-backed, unrecognized Artsakh Republic, have been without access to food, medication, and other essential goods and services since December 2022, when the Lachin Corridor, the only road connecting the region to Armenia, was blockaded. At least one person has died due to starvation caused by the blockade and many more are at risk. In 2020, the region saw the worst flare up in conflict since Armenia and Azerbaijan fought a major border war from 1992-1994. Since the previous conflict ended in 2020, the region has seen intermittent levels of violence.

A 19 August fire in the Malicha IDP site in central South Kivu province in eastern DRC has affected over 84,000 people and left over 40,000 people in the camp homeless. Access to shelter and clean water are the primary concerns currently.

On 8 September, a 6.8 magnitude earthquake struck the High Atlas Mountain range near Marrakech, affecting at least 300,000 people. The earthquake and subsequent aftershocks have killed at least 1,300 people and injured at least 1,800. Figures are likely to be higher as the impacts of the earthquake become clearer, and we will update this classification to reflect updated figures in the coming days.

On 10 September, Storm Daniel made landfall in Libya, causing severe weather conditions, including strong winds and sudden heavy rainfall affecting several northeastern areas of Libya (Benghazi, Tobruk, Toukra, Talmeitha, Almarj, Taknes (Al Jabal Al Akhdar), Al Owailia, Bayada, Albayda, Shahhat, Sousa and Derna). While the precise impacts of the flooding are still being assessed, at least 2,000 people have reportedly died, while some 10,000 are reported missing. Moreover, at least 2,050 people have been displaced, though actual figures are likely higher. The storm reportedly led to significant infrastructure damage, including road network, disrupted the telecommunications network, while two dams also collapsed, leading to further flooding and damage. We will continue to update this classification as updated data about the humanitarian impacts becomes available.

On 6 September, Storm Daniel made landfall in Greece, particularly impacting the Thessaly Region, which is home to 25% of the country's agriculture. At least 17 people were killed and 50,000 affected.

On 10 September, Storm Daniel made landfall in Libya, causing severe weather conditions, including strong winds and sudden heavy rainfall affecting several northeastern areas of Libya (Benghazi, Tobruk, Toukra, Talmeitha, Almarj, Taknes (Al Jabal Al Akhdar), Al Owailia, Bayada, Albayda, Shahhat, Sousa and Derna). While the precise impacts of the flooding are still being assessed, at least 5,300 people have reportedly died, while some 10,000 are reported missing. Moreover, at least 36,000 people have been displaced, though actual figures are likely higher. The storm reportedly led to significant infrastructure damage, including road network, disrupted the telecommunications network, while two dams also collapsed, leading to further flooding and damage. We will continue to update this classification as updated data about the humanitarian impacts becomes available.

Since June, heavy rains and flooding have afffected over 1,000 people Northwest Ecuador. Severe rains are expected to continue due to the impacts of El Nino, particularly affecting the provinces of Manabi, Los Rios, Guayas, and Esmeraldas.

On 10 September, Storm Daniel made landfall in Libya, causing severe weather conditions, including strong winds and sudden heavy rainfall affecting several northeastern areas of Libya (Benghazi, Tobruk, Toukra, Talmeitha, Almarj, Taknes (Al Jabal Al Akhdar), Al Owailia, Bayada, Albayda, Shahhat, Sousa and Derna). While the precise impacts of the flooding are still being assessed, at least 11,300 people have reportedly died, while some 10,100 are reported missing. Moreover, at least 36,000 people have been displaced, though actual casualty and displacement figures are likely higher. The storm reportedly led to significant infrastructure damage, including road network, disrupted the telecommunications network, while two dams also collapsed, leading to further flooding and damage. We will continue to update this classification as updated data about the humanitarian impacts becomes available.

On 8 September, a 6.8 magnitude earthquake struck the High Atlas Mountain range near Marrakech, affecting at least 380,000 people. The earthquake and subsequent aftershocks have killed nearly 3,000 people and injured at least 5,600. Most fatalities are reported in Al Haouz municipality while the rest are reported in Ouarzazate, Azilal, Chichaoua and Taroudant provinces.

Since early July, over 60,000 refugees and migrants have arrived in Southern Italy, particularly to the island of Lampedusa. The majority of refugees and migrants are coming from West Africa, Syria, Bangladesh and Pakistan, and are travelling through Tunisia. Needs include shelter, food, and protection.

Over 13,500 ethnic Armenians have fled Nagorno-Karabakh, a region contested by Azerbaijan and the Armenian-backed, unrecognized Artsakh Republic, after Azerbaijan seized control of the territory in a military offensive. More people are expected to leave Nagorno Karabakh in the coming days and we will update the classification as new information emerges.

Over 28,000 ethnic Armenians have fled Nagorno-Karabakh, a region contested by Azerbaijan and the Armenian-backed, unrecognized Artsakh Republic, after Azerbaijan seized control of the territory in a military offensive. More people are expected to leave Nagorno Karabakh in the coming days and we will update the classification as new information emerges.

Since late June, over 85,000 people have been displaced and over 1,400 killed in the Sagaing and Magway regions of northern Myanmar. Airstrikes, arson, IEDs, and other violence targeting civilians continues in these regions while humanitarian access is particularly limited.

Since 5 August, over 1.3 million people have been affected  and over 130,000 displaced by  flash flooding in the Chittagong division in the south east part of Bangladesh. Cox's Bazaar has been particularly impacted, with over 85,000 people displaced within the settlement.

Since July, over 160,000 migrants and refugees have arrived in Southern Mexico, hoping to eventually migrate further north to the US border, and overwhelming shelters and processing offices. Migrants and refugees face serious protection risks, in particular due to overcrowding at shelters, forcing people to set up makeshift camps on the street. Further arrivals are expected in the coming days.

Over 47,000 ethnic Armenians have fled Nagorno-Karabakh, a region contested by Azerbaijan and the Armenian-backed, unrecognized Artsakh Republic, after Azerbaijan seized control of the territory in a military offensive. More people are expected to leave Nagorno Karabakh in the coming days and we will update the classification as new information emerges.

Over 91,000 ethnic Armenians have fled Nagorno-Karabakh, a region contested by Azerbaijan and the Armenian-backed, unrecognized Artsakh Republic, after Azerbaijan seized control of the territory in a military offensive. We anticipate that more ethnic Armenians will leave Nagorno-Karabakh in the coming days.

A major new conflict has broken out in Gaza and southern Israel. The situation is evolving rapidly after armed actors from Gaza launched a major raid and fired hundreds of rockets into southern Israel on 7 October. Israel has since launched airstrikes on Gaza, killing at least 198 and injuring more than 1,600 Palestinians. The Orange response stance signals our assessment of risk for escalating violence and humanitarian suffering, and intent to mobilize proportional action. The 1-10 severity score is based solely on data about how civilians have been affected and, since such information is currently very limited, the score remains low for now. The Global Crisis Analysis team is working closely with MENA colleagues to track the situation, and we will update this classification as more data becomes available.

Since 05 October, continuous shelling and airstrikes in Idleb and Aleppo governorates in Northwest Syria have displaced at least 25,000 people, most from the towns of Jisr-Ash-Shugur, Samrin, Idleb, and Daret Azza. Additionally, at least 36 people have been killed with over 200 injured. The attacks have affected critical facilities and infrastructure including the main power station in Idleb city, schools, health facilities and camps, and has led to some humanitarian actors suspending their activities.

A 6.3 magnitude earthquake struck near the western city of Herat on 7 October. Government officials say that at least 2,000 people have been killed, 9,000 injured and thousands more have lost their homes, particularly in the 14 worst-affected villages. There has already been one aftershock of 5.5 magnitude and many residents are staying outside for fear of further damage. We will update this classification as more information becomes available about the impacts of the quake.

A major new conflict has broken out in Gaza and southern Israel. The situation is evolving rapidly after armed actors from Gaza launched a major raid and fired hundreds of rockets into southern Israel on 7 October. Israel has since launched airstrikes on Gaza, killing at least 413 and injuring more than 2,300 Palestinians. More than 123,000 people have also been displaced within Gaza. The Orange response stance signals our assessment of risk for escalating violence and humanitarian suffering, and intent to mobilize proportional action. The 1-10 severity score is based solely on data about how civilians have been affected and has increased as more data has become available about the humanitarian impacts of the situation. The Global Crisis Analysis team is working closely with MENA colleagues to track the situation, and we will update this classification as more data becomes available.

On 5 October, a rise in the water of level of Cameroon's Lagdo Dam caused extensive flooding across Nigeria's Adamawa state. The flooding exacerbated earlier flooding due to the recent release of water from the dam - including damage to properties and displacement. Out of 21 LGAs in Adamawa state, 14 LGAs have been affected by the floods and windstorms. 33 deaths were recorded, with 504 households comprising 51,043 individuals affected across 214 communities. 81 IDP camps were established across Yola South, Yola North, Lamude, Madagali and Demsa LGAs respectively.

A major new conflict has broken out in Gaza and southern Israel. The situation is evolving rapidly after armed actors from Gaza launched a major raid and fired hundreds of rockets into southern Israel on 7 October. Israel has since launched airstrikes on Gaza, killing at least 765 and injuring more than 4,000 Palestinians. More than 185,000 people have also been displaced within Gaza. The Orange response stance signals our assessment of risk for escalating violence and humanitarian suffering, and intent to mobilize proportional action. The 1-10 severity score is based solely on data about how civilians have been affected and has increased as more data has become available about the humanitarian impacts of the situation. The Global Crisis Analysis team is working closely with MENA colleagues to track the situation, and we will update this classification as more data becomes available.

A major new conflict has broken out in Gaza and southern Israel. The situation is evolving rapidly after armed actors from Gaza launched a major raid and fired hundreds of rockets into southern Israel on 7 October. Israel has since launched airstrikes on Gaza, killing at least 1,400 and injuring more than 6,000 Palestinians. More than 420,000 people have also been displaced within Gaza. Additionally, needs in Gaza are likely to deepen due to the lack of access to food, water, and fuel caused by the siege there. The Red response stance signals our assessment of risk for escalating violence and humanitarian suffering, and intent to mobilize proportional action. The 1-10 severity score is based solely on data about how civilians have been affected and has increased as more data has become available about the humanitarian impacts of the situation. The Global Crisis Analysis team is working closely with MENA colleagues to track the situation, and we will update this classification as more data becomes available.

Since August, over 2,000 people have been displaced throughout the Mopti region of central Mali due to increased activity by armed groups. Most have been displaced from the town of Tenekou and are in need of shelter, food, and protection services.

A major new conflict has broken out in Gaza and southern Israel. The situation is evolving rapidly after armed actors from Gaza launched a major raid and fired hundreds of rockets into southern Israel on 7 October. Israel has since launched airstrikes on Gaza, killing at least 2,300 and injuring more than 8,700 Palestinians. More than 1 million people have also been displaced within Gaza, following Israel's announcement to evacuate all of northern Gaza. Additionally, needs in Gaza are likely to deepen due to the lack of access to food, water, and fuel caused by the siege there.

Since August, over 100,000 Afghan refugees have returned from Iran and Pakistan to Afghanistan, primarly to Kabul, Herat, Nangarhar, and Khost provinces. This is largely due to policy changes in Pakistan and Iran, and more Afghans are expected to return in the coming days.

On 10 September, Storm Daniel made landfall in Libya, causing severe weather conditions, including strong winds and sudden heavy rainfall affecting several northeastern areas of Libya (Benghazi, Tobruk, Toukra, Talmeitha, Almarj, Taknes (Al Jabal Al Akhdar), Al Owailia, Bayada, Albayda, Shahhat, Sousa and Derna). At least 4,300 people confirmed dead, while some 8,500 are still missing. Moreover, at least 43,000 people have been displaced. The storm led to significant infrastructural damage, including road networks, and disrupted the telecommunications networks, while two dams also collapsed, leading to further flooding and damage.

On 18 October, Burkina Faso declared a Dengue fever outbreak after an extreme spike in cases and deaths over the past few weeks. Since August, over 40,000 cases have been reported with at least 100 deaths. Over half of these cases have been reported in the past month. The outbreak is concentrated in the urban centers of the capital OUagadougou, and Bobo Dioulasso.

Since the escalation in Israel and Gaza began on 7 October, southern Lebanon has witnessed a deterioration in the security situation as well, with hostilities taking place between armed groups and Israeli forces along the border. Artillery fire has led to the destruction of civilian housing and critical infrastructure, leading to over 20,000 displacements. The situation is evolving, and we will work with colleagues in the MENA region to monitor the situation and reclassify as needed.

A major new conflict has broken out in Gaza and southern Israel. The situation is evolving rapidly after armed actors from Gaza launched a major raid and fired hundreds of rockets into southern Israel on 7 October, which killed at least 1,400 people and
 injured over 4,000 more. At least 200,000 people have been displaced from southern Israel as well as the northern border with Lebanon. The impact of the conflict within Gaza is being classified separately.

On 25 October, Hurricane Otis made landfall over the city of Acapulco on Mexico's western coast, causing severe damage and affecting nearly 1 million people. Additionally, no fuel is available in the city due to damage from the storm, 42% of the population is without electricity, and two hospitals have been severely damaged.

Since August, over 190,000 Afghan refugees have returned from Iran and Pakistan to Afghanistan, primarily to Kabul, Herat, Nangarhar, and Khost provinces. This is largely due to policy changes in Pakistan and Iran, and more Afghans are expected to return in the coming days as the Government of Pakistan has issued a 31 October deadline for "undocumented" Afghan migrants and refugees to leave the country.

On 24 October, tropical cyclone Hamoon made landfall over the southeastern coast of Bangladesh affecting Cox's Bazar and the surrounding areas. Over 400,000 people are affected with over more than 37,000 homes destroyed. Needs include water, food and protection.

Since the beginning of conflict between Israel and Palestinian armed groups in Gaza on 7 October, the West Bank has also seen an increase in violence. This has displaced over 800 people, with nearly 2,300 civilian casualties, including more than 100 deaths. Please note that this classification focuses only on the impacts in the West Bank, and we have classified the situation in Gaza is separately.

Since October, heavy rains led to rising water levels in the Juba and Shabelle rivers, triggering flooding that has affected over 700,000 people in central and southern Somalia and displaced over 110,000 more. South West and Jubaland states, in the southern part of the country, are the worst affected, although Puntland, Galmudug, and Hirshabelle are also affected. On November 5, heavy rains associated with El Nino further intensified the situation.

A new front of fighting between Myanmar government forces and armed groups started in late October in Northern Shan state. The fighting has since spread to the surrounding states, including Sagaing, Southern Shan, Kayah, Chin, Rakhine, and Kayin. Over 200,000 people have been displaced due to the escalation in conflict, with over 90 others injured. The ongoing conflict and access restrictions are inhibiting displaced people's ability to find shelter and essential services.

Since October, heavy rains have led to rising water levels in the Juba and Shabelle rivers, triggering flooding that has affected over 1.24 million people in central and southern Somalia and displaced over 450,000. South West and Jubaland states, in the southern part of the country, are the worst affected, although Puntland, Galmudug, and Hirshabelle are also affected. The rains, associated with El Nino, have intensified in November and are expected to continue throughout the month.

A 6.3 magnitude earthquake struck near the western city of Herat on 7 October. Government officials say that at least 2,200 people have been killed, 9,000 injured. At least 10,000 homes have been completely destroyed, leaving thousands homeless, particularly in the 14 worst-affected villages. There has already been one aftershock of 5.5 magnitude and many residents are staying outside for fear of further damage.

Since August, over 14,000 people have been displaced and nearly 400 killed in Magway state of central Myanmar. Airstrikes, arson, IEDs, and other violence targeting civilians continues in while humanitarian access is particularly limited.

A major new conflict that broke out in Gaza and southern Israel has continued. The situation is evolving rapidly after armed actors from Gaza launched a major raid and fired hundreds of rockets into southern Israel on 7 October, which killed at least 1,200 people and injured nearly 7,000 more. At least 200,000 people have been displaced from southern Israel as well as the northern border with Lebanon. The impacts of the conflict within Gaza, the West Bank, and southern Lebanon are being classified separately.

Since the escalation in Israel and Gaza began on 7 October, southern Lebanon has witnessed a deterioration in the security situation as well, with hostilities taking place between armed groups and Israeli forces along the border. Artillery fire has led to the destruction of civilian housing and critical infrastructure, leading to over 46,000 displacements. The situation is evolving and we will work with colleagues in the MENA region to monitor the situation and reclassify as needed.

A new front of fighting between Myanmar government forces and armed groups started in late October in Northern Shan state. The fighting has since spread to the surrounding states, including southern Shan, Kayah, Rakhine, Chin, Sagaing, Mandalay, eastern Bago, Kayin, Mon, and Tanintharyi. Over 335,000 people have been displaced due to the escalation in conflict, with nearly 200 civilian casualties reported. The ongoing conflict and access restrictions are inhibiting displaced people's ability to find shelter and essential services, while the security situation is rapidly deteriorating.

Since September, over 413,000 Afghan refugees have returned from Iran and Pakistan to Afghanistan, primarily to Kabul, Herat, Nangarhar, and Khost provinces. This is largely due to policy changes in Pakistan and Iran, and more Afghans are expected to return. Returnees are facing urgent needs, particularly as they experience winter conditions in some locations.

Heavy rains, associated with El Nino, have caused flooding across 36 of Kenya's 47 counties and resulted in over 340,000 people displaced. Garissa, Mandera, and Wajir counties in northeast Kenya are the most affected, although flooding is also affecting several areas in the Northwest, Central Highlands, and Southeast lowlands as well. The flooding has also resulted in a spike of cholera cases, particularly in Dadaab refugee camp in Garissa county, which has seen over 5,000 cases since late September.

A major new conflict has broken out in Gaza and southern Israel. The situation evolved rapidly after armed actors from Gaza launched a major raid and fired hundreds of rockets into southern Israel on 7 October. Israel has since launched airstrikes on Gaza, killing at least 14,500 and injuring more than 36,000 Palestinians. More than 1.7 million people, about 80% of the population, have also been displaced within Gaza. Additionally, needs in Gaza are likely to deepen due to the lack of access to food, water, and fuel caused by the siege there.

Since 16 October, Yemen has seen a spike in cholera cases, primarily in the south and eastern parts of the country. Shabwah, Hadramout, Al Mukalla, Aden, Abyan, Lahj, and Taiz governorates all have confirmed cholera cases with most located in Shabwah. About 30% of all cases are in children under five.

Over 450,000 people have been displaced since 1 October due to clashes between the M23 armed group and the Congolese armed forces (FARDC) in North Kivu in the eastern DRC. Most are displaced from the territories of Masisi, Nyiragongo, and Rutshuru. Clashes continue to threaten civilians lives and limit humanitarian access throughout North Kivu.

Widespread conflict over grazing land and water rights in Benue State in Southern Nigeria has resulted in over 9,000 people displaced in the past three months. The impacts of climate change, which have obstructed grazing routes and disrupted livestock and cropping systems, has further intensified the situation. Additionally, the insecurity has resulted in deaths, injuries, and destruction of property.

Heavy rainfall, associated with El Nino, caused flooding and landslides in the Dar es Salaam and Manyara regions of northern Tanzania. The flooding has affected over 15,000 people and damaged nearly 1,300 homes. Flooding has also resulted in contaminated water sources, heightening the risk of waterborne diseases.

Soum province in the Sahel region of northern Burkina Faso has been experiencing a deterioration in the security situation. In addition to the capital of Soum, D'jibo, being besieged for over a year, there has been an increase in attacks by non-state armed groups. An attack in late November on D'jibo has resulted in over 210 deaths and at least 2,400 displacements in Soum.

Since the beginning of conflict between Israel and Palestinian armed groups in Gaza on 7 October, the West Bank has also seen an increase in violence. This has displaced over 1,000 people, with nearly 3,900 civilian casualties, including more than 270 deaths. Please note that this classification focuses only on the impacts in the West Bank, and we have classified the situation in Gaza is separately.

A new front of fighting between Myanmar government forces and armed groups started in late October in Northern Shan state. The fighting has since spread to the surrounding states, including southern Shan, Kayah, Rakhine, Chin, Sagaing, Mandalay, eastern Bago, Kayin, Mon, and Tanintharyi. Over 660,000 people have been displaced due to the escalation in conflict, with over 370 civilian casualties reported. The ongoing conflict and access restrictions are inhibiting displaced people's ability to find shelter and essential services, while the security situation is rapidly deteriorating.

Soum province in the Sahel region of northern Burkina Faso has been experiencing a deterioration in the security situation. In addition to the capital of Soum, Djibo, facing continued siege, there have been increased attacks by non-state armed groups. Attacks by armed groups in November and December 2023 have killed almost 250 people and displaced more than 3,600.

On 18 October, Burkina Faso declared a Dengue fever outbreak after an extreme spike in cases and deaths in early October. Since late October, over 96,000 cases have been reported with at least 474 deaths. The outbreak is concentrated in the urban centers of the capital Ouagadougou, and Bobo Dioulasso.

Since the escalation in Israel and Gaza began on 7 October, southern Lebanon has witnessed a deterioration in the security situation as well, with hostilities taking place between armed groups and Israeli forces along the border. Artillery fire has destroyed civilian housing and critical infrastructure, displacing over 74,000 people.

Nearly one million people are facing IPC4+ levels of food insecurity in Jonglei, Unity, Upper Nile, and Northern Bahr el Ghazal states of northern South Sudan. 25,000 people, including refugees fleeing Sudan, are facing the most extreme level of food insecurity, IPC 5, most of whom are in Jonglei. Food insecurity is being driven by the depreciation of the local currency, spillover effects of the conflict in Sudan,  and lower-than-average agricultural production. The situation is expected to worsen in the coming months.

Since 23 December, armed groups have been attacking villages within Bokkos Local Government Area  (LGA) in Plateau State in Central Nigeria. Over 13,500 people have been displaced and 165 killed. Attacks are ongoing and we expect to see further displacement.

Since October 7, Israel has launched airstrikes in Gaza, killing at least 23,200 and injuring more than 56,000 Palestinians, in response to the attacks in Southern Israel. More than 1.9 million people, about 85% of the population, have also been displaced within Gaza. Additionally, needs in Gaza are likely to deepen due to the lack of access to food, water, and fuel caused by the siege there.

Over 325,000 people have been displaced since November 2023 due to renewed clashes between the M23 armed group and the Congolese armed forces (FARDC) in North Kivu in eastern DRC starting in October. Additionally, nearly 800,000 people in North Kivu are experiencing IPC4 levels of food insecurity, while over 7,000 cases of cholera have been reported.

Heavy rainfall since early December has caused the Congo River to rise to its highest level in 60 years, resulting in flooding across the Democratic Republic of the Congo. Flooding has affected at least 15 of the DRC's 26 provinces, most heavily impacting Equateur and Kinshasa provinces in the western part of the country. At least 304,000 families have been impacted, and 300 people killed.

Since early December, over 335,000 people have been affected and 25,000 displaced by flooding in 9 of the 12 departments of the Republic of Congo due to heavy rains causing the Congo River to overflow. The rainfall has been twice as heavy as the usual average, causing the Congo River to rise to its highest level in 60 years. The impacts of flooding in the DRC are being classified separately

Heavy rains, associated with El Nino, have caused flooding across 36 of Kenya's 47 counties and resulted in over 540,000 people affected. Garissa, Mandera, and Wajir counties in northeast Kenya are the most affected, although flooding has also affected several areas in the Northwest, Central Highlands, and Southeast lowlands as well. While the rains have subsided, at least 67,000 people remain displaced.

In late December 2023, an armed group launched an attack in the village of Nzakoundou, in Ouham-Pendé prefecture in the northwest part of Central African Republic. The attacks displaced over 3,500 people, killing at least 22, and injuring 25 others.

Since late December, villages throughout the Est region of Burkina Faso have come under attack by non-state armed groups. Over, 6,560 people have been displaced by the violence, with most going to Fada N'gourma commune in the western part of Est region.

Fighting between Sudan's military (SAF) and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) has continued since April 2023.  In the past three months, over 1.2 million people have been newly displaced due to the conflict, with over 2,300 others killed. Additionally, at least 4.8 million people are experiencing IPC4 levels of food insecurity. Both fighting and food insecurity are largely concentrated in Khartoum and Darfur. Note, this emergency was previously classified as SD294 and is being reclassified with a new code as per the classification guidance.

Since October 7, Israel has launched airstrikes and ground invasions in Gaza. In the past three months alone, at least 33,000 people have been injured and nearly 15,000 killed, with more affected since the start of the conflict. At least 1.7 million people remain displaced. Additionally, over 1 million people are facing emergency (IPC4) levels of food insecurity with 570,000 facing catastrophic (IPC5) levels of food insecurity.

In early February, attacks by armed actors in Manyu division in Southwest Cameroon resulted in at least 3 deaths and over 4,300 people displaced. About half the population displaced remained in the Manyu division of Southwest Cameroon, while others fled to the neighboring Momo division of Northwest Cameroon.

Since late December 2023, nearly 300 cases of meningitis - resulting in at least 52 deaths - have been recorded in Yobe state of Northeast Nigeria. Most of the cases reported are in children between the ages of 5 and 18.

Over 750,000 people have been displaced since December 2023 due to renewed clashes between the M23 armed group and the Congolese armed forces (FARDC) in North Kivu in the eastern DRC. In the past month, fighting has intensified around Masisi territory, particularly in the city of Sake, only 25km from the provincial capital, Goma. Additionally, over 700,000 people are facing IPC4 levels of food insecurity, while over 3,000 cholera cases have been reported in the same time period.

Since December, attacks by armed groups have displaced nearly 39,000 people in Ouest Department of Haiti, which includes the capital city, Port-au-Prince. Nearly 15,000 of those people were displaced in attacks in late February. Additionally, schools, businesses, and many public services are closed due to the ongoing insecurity in Port-au-Prince.

Since December, flood in the Fouli department of the Lac province in western Chad has affected nearly 10,000 people, most of whom are internally diplsaced. The flooding was caused by the collapse of five dams and has additionally resulted in the destruction of crops and agricultural land.

A new wave of attacks by armed groups in Mozambique's northern Cabo Delgado province has resulted in nearly 100,000 people displaced, including at least 60,000 children. The attacks were concentrated in the southern part of Cabo Delgado, which has historically been considered safe from armed groups. The attacks come as the South African Development Community's mission in Mozambique is in the process of withdrawing from the province.

Since January, attacks by armed groups have displaced over 35,000 in Ouest Department of Haiti, which includes the capital city, Port-au-Prince. Nearly 15,000 of those people were displaced in attacks in late February. Additonally, over 760,000 peoplein Ouest are experiencing IPC4 levels of food insecurity.

Since October 7, Israel has launched airstrikes and ground invasions in Gaza. In the past three months alone, at least 9,000 people have been injured and nearly 5,700 killed, with more affected since the start of the conflict. At least 1.7 million people remain displaced. Additionally, over 1.1 million people are facing Catastrophe (IPC 5) levels of food insecurity, with an additional 854,000 facing emergency (IPC 4).

Since January 2024, over 6,000 cases of cholera have been reported in northern Yemen, with at least 44 associated deaths. Over 4,000 of these cases have been reported in the past few weeks alone and the outbreak is expected to worsen as the rainy season is beginning.

Since late February, at least 8 fires have broken out in multiple IDP camps across Borno state, affecting over 30,000 people and killing 24. In addition, over 200,000 people, most of whom are internally displaced, are experiencing IPC4 levels of food insecurity in Borno.

On 03 April, a 7.4 magnitude earthquake struck Taiwan's eastern coast, just 25km away from the rural and mountainous Hualien County. Tremors were felt throughout the entire island, including the capital city Taipei, and has resulted in at least 1,000 injuries and 10 deaths, with dozens more still missing.

Since February, heavy rainfall has caused flooding and landslides across most of Burundi. The western provinces of Bujumbura Mairie (where the capital city Bujumbura is located), Rumonge, Makamba, and Cibitoke are most affected. Since February, nearly 14,000 people have been displaced with over 70,000 affected.

Since April 13, heavy rains have caused flash floods throughout 23 provinces in eastern Afghanistan. More than 1,200 homes have been destroyed by the flooding, leaving thousands displaced and at least 60 people killed. The rainfall is unseasonal for this time of year and comes after a particularly dry winter. More rains and floods are expected in the coming days.

Heavy rainfall has caused the overflow of rivers and lakes in Tanganyika province in the south-eastern part of the DRC, resulting in extensive floods. Flooding has affected at least 50,000 people and destroyed thousands of homes. In addition, nearly 300,000 people are experiencing IPC4 levels of food insecurity in the parts of Tanganyika that are particularly affected by floods, Nyunzu and Kalemie.

There has been a spike in Monkeypox cases in the central and northern regions of the DRC since the start of the year, with nearly 3,000 cases reported since mid-February. While cases have been reported in 23 of the 26 provinces across the country, the majority of cases are located in Equateur.  Children younger than 15 years old account for 70% of the total cases and 88% of deaths.

Fighting between Sudan's military (SAF) and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) has continued since April 2023.  In the past three months, more than 600,000 people have been newly displaced due to the conflict, with nearly 2,500 others killed. Additionally, over 4.8 million people are experiencing IPC4 levels of food insecurity, though more updated IPC assessments have been unavailable due to security and access constraints. The food insecurity situation is expected to worsen in the coming months as Sudan enters the lean season.

Since early April, heavy rains across Somalia but primarily in Hirshabelle, South West, and Banadir states, have affected over 127,000 people and displaced more than 8,000. The rains are expected to worsen and continue throughout the Gu, or rainy, season lasting until June.

Since 23 April, heavy rains and flash floods in Kenya associated with El Nino have displaced over 150,000 people and killed at least 103. Flooding has affected all 47 counties of Kenya, but the capital city of Nairobi is particularly impacted, with at least 100,000 people displaced in Nairobi alone. More rainfall is expected in the coming days.

On 13 April, armed clashes between the Tigray's People Liberation Front (TPLF) and Amharic forces   in Alamata Town, and Raya Alamata, Zala, and Ofla woredas in Northern Ethiopia, have displaced at least 50,000 people.  Raya Alamata is a contested area claimed by both Amhara and Tigray.

In early April, armed clashes between armed actors and communities in Jor woreda, in eastern Ethiopia bordering South Sudan, displaced over 6,000 people. In addition, at least 61 people were killed due to the fighting.

Sinc late January, Somali and Oromia regions have been experiencing a spike in cholera cases, with at least 8,700 cases reported in the last three months. Various regions in Ethiopia have reported cholera cases since 2022, but the past three months represent a 55% increase in cases from the months before. Additionally, there are currently cases in other regions, but the current spike is primarily in Somali and Oromia.

Insecurity is increasing in Northwest Nigeria due to conflict between armed groups. In Zamfara and Sokoto states, armed attacks have displaced at least 10,600 people and killed at least 92 since March. Additionally, many others have been kidnapped throughout these attacks.

In Mali's eastern Menaka region, at least 13,700 people have been displaced due to attacks on supply convoys and increasing conflict activity since January. In addition, the region has been effectively blockaded since April 2023, resulting in increased food insecurity.

Since March 2024, over 34,000 cases of cholera have been reported in Yemen, with at least 150 associated deaths. Cases are reported in at least 20 governorates across the country, although most are concentrated in Amran and Sana'a in the north.

Since April 13, heavy rains have caused flash floods throughout 23 provinces in Northeastern Afghanistan, with severe flooding most recently occuring on May 11. More than 3,300 homes have been destroyed by the flooding, leaving thousands displaced, and at least 360 people killed. The rainfall is unseasonal for this time of year and comes after a particularly dry winter. More rains and floods are expected in the coming days.

Since March, heavy rainfall has caused flooding throughout southern Yemen, affecting over 11,000 people, many of whom were already internally displaced. More rainfall and flooding is expected in the coming days.

On May 20, over 4,500 people were displaced from Gujba LGA in Yobe state in northeast Nigeria due to attacks from members of non-state armed groups. Many of the people displaced have been displaced previously from Borno state and have urgent needs including shelter, protection, and health services.

On 26 May, tropical Cyclone Remal made landfall on the coast of Bangladesh, affecting over 3.7 million people, and destroying over 35,000 homes. The cyclone affected 19 of Bangladesh's 64 districts, most severely affecting those on the coast such as Bagerhat, Barisal, Bhola, Khulna, and Patuakhali.

Since March, heavy rainfall has caused flooding and landslides across most of Burundi. The western provinces of Bujumbura Mairie (where the capital city Bujumbura is located), Rumonge, Makamba, and Cibitoke are most affected. Since March, over 23,000 people have been displaced with over 140,000 affected.

Since October 7, Israel has launched airstrikes and ground invasions in Gaza. In the past three months alone, nearly 6,000 people have been killed and over 9,000 injured, with more affected since the start of the conflict. Since the start of the offensive in Rafah in early May, over 1 million people have been displaced, the vast majority from Rafah. At least 1.7 million people remain displaced. Additionally, over 1.1 million people are facing Catastrophe (IPC 5) levels of food insecurity, with an additional 854,000 facing emergency (IPC 4).

On 26 May, tropical Cyclone Remal made landfall on the coast of Bangladesh, affecting over 4.6 million people, and destroying over 40,000 homes. The cyclone affected 19 of Bangladesh's 64 districts, most severely affecting those on the coast such as Bagerhat, Barisal, Bhola, Brguna, Khulna, and Patuakhali.

In Mali's eastern Menaka region, at least 15,200 people have been displaced due to attacks on supply convoys and increasing conflict activity since April 2024. In addition, the region has been effectively blockaded for over a year, resulting in increased food insecurity, with at least 67,000 experiencing IPC4 and over 2,500 people experiencing the most extreme level of food insecurity, IPC5. Food insecurity is expected to worsen with the start of the lean season and as insecurity is worsening.

Since March, attacks by armed groups have displaced over 215,000 from Ouest Department of Haiti, which includes the capital city, Port-au-Prince. Most have remained in Ouest department or fled to neighboring Grand Sud department. Additionally, over 1 million people are experiencing IPC4 levels of food insecurity in Ouest and Grand Sud.

Since late May, nearly 15,000 people have been displaced in Gnagna province in eastern Burkina Faso due to threats of violence and eviction by armed groups. Access in this part of Burkina Faso remains extremely limited.

Heavy rainfall has caused the overflow of rivers and lakes in Tanganyika province in the south-eastern part of the DRC, resulting in extensive floods. Flooding has affected at least 250,000 people and destroyed thousands of homes. In addition, over 418,000 people are experiencing IPC4 levels of food insecurity in Tanganyika province.

Since 23 April, heavy rains and flash floods in Kenya associated with El Nino have displaced over 300,000 people and killed at least 315. Flooding has affected all 47 counties of Kenya, but the capital city of Nairobi is particularly impacted, with at least 100,000 people displaced in Nairobi alone.

Since early April, heavy rains across Somalia but primarily in Hirshabelle, South West, and Banadir states, have affected over 226,000 people and displaced more than 38,000. In addition, nearly 8,000 cases of cholera have been reported since the rains began. The rains are expected to worsen and continue throughout the Gu, or rainy, season lasting through June.

Since late March, Somali and Oromia regions have been experiencing a spike in cholera cases, with at least 8,500 cases reported in the last three months. Various regions in Ethiopia have reported cholera cases since 2022, but the past three months represent a 55% increase in cases from the months before. Additionally, there are currently cases in other regions, but the current spike is primarily in Somali and Oromia.

Since late March, over 61,000 cases of cholera have been reported in  Yemen, with at least 240 associated deaths. Cases are reported in at least 20 governorates across the country, although most are concentrated in Amran and Sana'a in the north.
In late June, heavy rain caused flooding and landslides in Cote d'Ivoire's largest city, Abidjan. Flooding and landslides displaced over 2,000 people and killed at least 24.

Since late June, flooding in Myanmar's northern Kachin State has displaced at least 16,000 people, many of whom were previously displaced due to conflict. Flooding has primarily affected Kachin state capital, Myitkyina, where thousands remain trapped in their homes.

Since late April, over 75,000 refugees from Sudan have fled to Libya as conditions in Sudan worsen. Most refugees are in Alkufra in southeastern Libya, which borders Sudan, and are in need of shelter, protection, and health services.

In southern Niger, particularly the regions of Diffa, Tillabery, and Tahoua, increasing insecurity and attacks by non-state armed groups have displaced nearly 14,000 people. In addition, flooding in these regions has left an additional 10,000 people displaced since late June.

Since October 2023, Israel has launched airstrikes and ground invasions in Gaza. In the past three months alone, nearly 4,500 people have been killed and over 11,700 injured, with more affected since the start of the conflict, though these figures are likely higher. At least 200,000 people have also been displaced within the past 90 days. Moreover, nearly 500,000 people are facing Catastrophe (IPC 5) levels of food insecurity, with an additional 745,000 facing emergency (IPC 4), while humanitarian access remains severely constrained.

Since late June, heavy rains have caused flooding throughout Montserrado county, which includes capitol city of Monrovia, in Liberia. Flooding as displaced at least 48,000 people and is expected to continue in the coming days.

Since April, over 86,000 refugees and returnees from Sudan have fled to South Sudan as conditions in Sudan worsen due to conflict there. Furthermore, nearly 100,000 returnees in South Sudan are facing IPC4 levels of food insecurity, with 28,000 facing catastrophic, or IPC5 levels of food insecurity.

A 15 July storm brought heavy rainfall to eastern Afghanistan, with Nangarhar province especially affected. At least 47 people have been killed and 350 injured, with more than 400 families affected. More information is emerging about the extent of the impacts.

The conflict in Sudan between Sudan's military (SAF) and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) has continued and intensified in parts of the country over the past three months. In the states generally controlled by the SAF, over 778,000 people have been displaced in the past three months, while over 4.5 million people are facing emergency or IPC 4 levels of food insecurity. Another 295,000 people are facing catastrophic or IPC 5 levels of food insecurity, particularly in Al Jazirah, Blue Nile, and Khartoum states.

The conflict in Sudan between Sudan's military (SAF) and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) has continued and intensified in parts of the country over the past three months, particularly in the Darfur and Kordofan states largely controlled by the RSF. Fighting in these states – including in North Darfur’s capital, el-Fasher – has displaced over 3 million people, and left nearly 4 million people facing emergency or IPC 4 levels of food insecurity, with nearly 500,000 facing catastrophic or IPC 5 food insecurity.

Since late May, over 54,000 people have been displaced and 106 people have died in Gnagna province in eastern Burkina Faso due to threats of violence and eviction by armed groups. Access in this part of Burkina Faso remains extremely limited.

Disagreements since late June over land use and access, including water points and natural resources, led to violent interactions between groups in Galdogob and Jariban districts in western Somalia, causing 8700 to be displaced and 60 people to be killed.
On 5 July, an armed conflict erupted in Luuq town in Jubaland state in southern Somalia, resulting in the death of 4 civilians and forced displacement of 42,000 people.

Since March, heavy rainfall has caused flooding and landslides across most of Burundi. The western provinces of Bujumbura Mairie (where the capital city Bujumbura is located), Rumonge, Makamba, and Cibitoke are most affected. Since March, over 49,000 people have been displaced with over 248869 affected. This is coinciding with the Minister of Health declaring an MPox epidemic on 25 July 2024. Confirmed cases are located in Bujumbura Nord (4), Isare (2), Gitega (1), and Nyanza-Lac (2).

In southern Niger, particularly the regions of Diffa, Tillabery, and Maradi, increasing insecurity and attacks by non-state armed groups have displaced nearly 29,568 people. In addition, flooding in these regions has left an additional 4,000 people displaced.

On 26 July, the Ministry of Health in the Central African Republic decladed an mpox epidemic, following a spike of cases throughout the country, including in the capital city of Bangui. Additionally, nearly 95,000 people are experiencing IPC4 levels of food insecurity, mostly in the western provinces which are also affected by mpox.

Since late May, over 7,000 mpox cases have been reported in DRC with at least 127 associated deaths, a significant increase from earlier in the year. While most cases are in the northern and central parts of the country, the outbreak is spreading the fastest in North and South Kivu in the eastern part of the DRC, which are affected by conflict. About 70% of cases are reported in children under 15. Mpox outbreaks in neighboring countries are being classified separately.

Since June, over 50,000 refugees from Sudan have fled to Libya as conditions in Sudan worsen. Most refugees are in Alkufra in southeastern Libya, which borders Sudan. Additionally, flooding on 11 August in Alkufra further displaced an additional 3,000 people, many of whom are Sudanese refugees.
Since the start of August, at least 129 cases of measles have been reported in Rafai, a town in the southern part of the Central African Republic, just north of the border with the DRC.
Since late July, flooding in Logone et Chari in the Far North region of Cameroon has affected nearly 75,000 people and displaced over 6,700.

The conflict in Sudan between Sudan's military (SAF) and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) has continued and intensified in parts of the country since April, particularly in the Darfur and Kordofan states largely controlled by the RSF. In the past three months, fighting in these states - including in North Darfur capital, el-Fasher - has displaced over 360,000 people, and left more than 3.9 million people in emergency or IPC4 levels of food insecurity, with nearly 460,000 people facing catastrophic or IPC5 food insecurity. Nearly half of the population is facing IPC 3 or higher food insecurity.

Since 21 August, flooding in eastern Bangladesh has affected over 3 million people, and displaced at least 60,000 across 11 districts withinn Chattogram and Sylhet divisions Flooding is expected to worsen in the coming days.

Since July 15, flooding has affected over 143,000 people throughout much of Nigeria, largely in the southern, central, and eastern parts of the country. More than 45,000 people have been displaced due to the floods

Since 8 July, at least 575,000 people have been displaced due to flooding in South Sudan. Over 570,000 of those dispaced were in Maiwut country of Upper Nile state in the northeast part of the country, although flooding also affected countries throughout Unity State as well.

Since May 2024, over 95,000 cases of cholera have been reported in  Yemen, with at least 217 associated deaths. Cases are reported in at least 20 governorates across the country, although most are concentrated in the north. Furthermore, flooding since June has displaced over 44,000 people, particularly in Al Hodeidah governorate in the western part of the country. Impacts from the flood are likely to worsen the ongoing cholera outbreak.

Since 21 August, flooding in eastern Bangladesh has affected over 5.5 million people, displaced at least 200,000, and left over 1 million people trapped in floodwaters across 12 districts within Chattogram and Sylhet divisions.
Since 22 July, severe flooding has affected 17 districts in Mali, affecting nearly 50,000 people, injuring 104, and killing 30.

Since 20 August, Nigeria has been inundated with flooding, starting in the northern parts of the country and spreading across much of the rest. More than 200k people have been  impacted by these floods. The flooding and its related destruction of homes and livelihoods is coinciding with cholera outbreaks.

An expanding MPox outbreak starting in the DRC has spread to Nigeria, so far impacting 10 new people since mid-June. Clade II is present in Nigeria, less servere than Clade Ib, which has been found in neighboring countries. Mpox outbreaks in other countries are being classified separately.

Pakistan has faced heavy flooding since 1 July 2024, primarily impacting Balochistan, in the SW of the country. As of 25 August, the floods have affected more than 42,000 people in the province and killed 21.
Since late July, flooding in the Far North region of Cameroon has affected over 158,000 people and displaced nearly 44,000.

Since July, heavy rains have triggered massive flooding across Chad, affecting over 960,000 people in all 23 provinces. Flooding has caused at least 175 deaths and displaced over 420,000 people. Refugees from Sudan in the eastern part of Chad are also impacted by the flooding.

Throughout Niger, flooding has displaced over 353,000 people and affected nearly 710,000, with Maradi, Zinder, and Tahoua in the southern part of the country being the most impacted. In addition, at least 18,795 people have been displaced due to insecurity in areas also impacted by flooding.

Since October 2023, Israel has launched airstrikes and ground invasions in Gaza. In the past three months alone, over 4,310 people have been killed and nearly 11,800 injured, with more affected since the start of the conflict, though these figures are likely higher. At least 330,000 people have also been displaced within the past 90 days. Moreover, nearly 500,000 people are facing Catastrophe (IPC 5) levels of food insecurity, with an additional 745,000 facing emergency (IPC 4), while humanitarian access remains severely constrained.

Like several other countries in the region, Côte d'Ivoire is experiencing an Mpox outbreak. Following the detection of the first case in Tabou health district in July 2024, cases have now been recorded in twenty other health districts in the country. As of 6 September, the health authorities have reported 45 confirmed cases and 1 death.

Fighting between Myanmar's military junta and multiple ethnic armed groups have displaced at least 300,000 people and killed over 5,000 in the past three months alone. In addition, flooding caused by Typhoon Yagi in Myanmar has resulted in over 277,000 additional displacements throughout central Myanmar.

Throughout Niger, flooding has displaced over 406people and affected nearly 710,000, with Maradi, Zinder, and Tahoua in the southern part of the country being the most impacted. In addition, at least 18,795 people have been displaced due to insecurity in areas also impacted by flooding.

On the 13th of August, a cholera alert was raised i Kesra sub-district, Deir-ez-Zor governorate in Northeast Syria. Since then, the local health authorities have reported at least 5 confirmed cases and 132 suspected cases. No deaths have been reported.
On 10 September, Central African Republic's (CAR) ministry of health declared a dengue fever outbreak, with at least 13 cases confirmed in the capital city, Bangui.

Since 20 August, Nigeria has been inundated with flooding, starting in the northern parts of the country and spreading across much of the rest. More than 1 million people have been  impacted by these floods. The 11 September break of the Alau Dam in Maiduguri, Borno state, has compounded the impacts of the flooding on livelihoods and health. The flooding is conciding with an outbreak of cholera, with at least 10,000 cases reported in the last three months.

Following torrential downpour in Central Europe over the past week, extensive flooding has hit the voivodships of Dolnoslaskie, Slaskie, and Opolskie in southwest Poland. The Polish Prime Minister declared a state of natural disaster in these voivodships on 16 September.So far, the flooding has displaced over 2,600 people.  One third of the residents of Wroclaw, in voivodship, are Ukrainian.

As the conflict intensifies and conditions worsen in Myanmar's Rakhine State, at least 20,000 Rohingya refugees have fled to neighboring Bangladesh.  In response, Bangladesh is tightening border security measure to prevent additional arrivals of refugees from Myanmar. Please note the conflict in Myanmar is classified separately.

Escalating tension between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon has resulted in a series of attacks and airstrikes throughout Lebanon. Israeli airstrikes have killed over 500 people, and injured thousands. Since early July, over 30,000 people have been displaced from southern Lebanon due to confrontations with Israel, including 16,000 since 23 September. The situation is evolving quickly, and we will continue to update the classification as needed.

Escalating tension between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon has resulted in a series of attacks and airstrikes throughout Lebanon. Israeli airstrikes have killed over 600 people, and injured thousands. Since early July, over 100,000 people have been displaced from southern Lebanon due to confrontation with Israel, including 90,530 since 23 September. The situation is evolving quickly and we will continue to update the classification as needed.

Due to Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon that began on 23 September, over 100,000 people have fled Lebanon to neighboring Syria. Most are crossing at Jdaidet Yabous border, about 45km west of Damascus. Please note the attacks and internal displacement in Lebanon are being classified separately.

Since late May, over 17,000 Mpox cases have been reported in DRC. While most cases are in the northern and central parts of the country, the outbreak is spreading the fastest in North and South Kivu in the eastern part of the DRC, which are affected by conflict.

In 2024, there have been 24,319 confirmed cases of cholera across the DRC with 321 deaths, with nearly 3,000 cases reported in the past 3 months. Most cases reoprted are in the eastern province of North Kivu, which is also experiencing conflict.  Recent floods across central and west Africa have increased the risk of cholera transmission.

The conflict in Sudan between Sudan's military (SAF) and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) has continued and intensified in parts of the country over the past three months. Another 295,000 people are facing catastrophic or IPC5 levels of food insecurity, particularly in Al Jazirah, Blue Nile, and Khartoum states. Please note conflict and food insecurity in RSF controlled areas of Sudan is classified separately.

Due to Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon that began on 23 September, over 300,000 people have fled Lebanon to neighboring Syria. Most are crossing at Jdaidet Yabous border, about 45km west of Damascus. Please note the attacks and internal displacement in Lebanon are being classified separately.

Escalating tension between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon has resulted in a series of attacks and airstrikes throughout Lebanon. Israeli airstrikes have killed over 1,900 people, and injured thousands more. Over one million people have been displaced since 23 September. The situation is evolving quickly and we will continue to update the classification as needed.

Nigeria is seeing a spike in cholera cases, with over 6,400 suspected cases and 232 deaths reported in the past three months. While about half of cases reported are in Lagos, Borno, Adamawa, and Yobe states in NE Nigeria are also affected.
Since 21 August, flooding has affected over 5.8 million people, displaced at least 500,000 others in eastern Bangladesh, including Cox's Bazar.
Since late July, flooding in the Far North region of Cameroon has affected over 200,000 people and displaced nearly 63,000.Flooding has limited access, particularly in Logone et Chari department.

Moreover, nearly 500,000 people are facing Catastrophe (IPC 5) levels of food insecurity, with an additional 745,000 facing emergency (IPC 4), while humanitarian access remains severely constrained.

On 27 September, Rwanda declared a Marburg Virus Disease (MVD) outbreak. Since then, at least 62 cases have been confirmed with 15 deaths, largely among health care workers in the country. While most cases have been reported in Kigali, there have been cases reported near the borders of Tanzania and the DRC, increasing risk of cross-border spread. MVD is a highly virulent disease similar to Ebola.

Throughout Niger, flooding has displaced nearly 900,000 people and affected nearly 1.5 million. In addition, at least 21,090 people have been displaced due to insecurity in areas also impacted by flooding.

Since August, monsoon rains have caused massive flooding throughout Pakistan, displacing nearly 600,000 people and killing at least 240. Flooding has damaged thousands of homes, critical infrastructure, and submerged farmland and livestock threatening food insecurity.

Since 23 September, over 16,700 people have fled the escalating crisis in Lebanon, through Syria to Iraq. Most are entering Iraq are through the Al-Qaim border crossing from Syria, although others are arriving via air into Baghdad. Over 60% of those arriving are women and children. Please note, the impacts of the crisis in Lebanon and Syria are classified separately.
Since late August, flooding in the Far North region of Cameroon has affected over 380,000 people and displaced nearly 274,000. Flooding has limited access, particularly in Logone et Chari department.

On the 13th of August, a cholera alert was raised i, Kesra sub-district, Deir-ez-Zor governorate in Northeast Syria. Since then, the local health authorities have reported 31 confirmed cases and 126 suspected cases. No deaths have been reported.

Since late August, attacks by armed groups have displaced over 140,000 people in South Kivu in the eastern DRC. Furthermore, over 135,000 people are experiencing IPC4 levels of food insecurity within South Kivu. Please note, the Mpox outbreak in the DRC, which is also present in South Kivu, is classified separately.

Escalating tension between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon has resulted in a series of attacks and airstrikes throughout Lebanon. Israeli airstrikes have killed over 2,500 people, and injured thousands more, and displaced over 780,000 since August. Please note, displacement from Lebanon to neighboring countries is being classified separately.

Since late August, over 22,000 Mpox cases have been reported in DRC. While most cases are in the northern and central parts of the country, the outbreak is spreading the fastest in North and South Kivu in the eastern part of the DRC, which are affected by conflict.

Due to Israeli airstrikes in Lebanon that began on 23 September, over 470,000 people have fled Lebanon to neighboring Syria, over half of whom are children. Please note the attacks and internal displacement in Lebanon are being classified separately.

On 14 November, Tropical Storm Sara made landfall in Honduras, affecting over 47,000 people. Rains are expected to continue throughout the week, increasing risk of further flooding and mudslides. Four Caribbean coastal departments, Atlántida, Yoro, Islas de la Bahía and Gracias a Dios, are most impacted by the storm, with hundreds of homes, buildings, and bridges damaged or destroyed.

Since early September, over 83,000 refugees from Sudan have fled to Libya as conditions in Sudan worsen. Most refugees are in Alkufra in southeastern Libya, which borders Sudan, and are in need of shelter, protection, and health services.

On 14 November, Tropical Storm Sara made landfall in Honduras, affecting over 250,000 people and displacing over 15,000. Rains are expected to continue throughout the week, increasing risk of further flooding and mudslides. Four Caribbean  coastal departments, Atlántida, Yoro, Islas de la Bahía and Gracias a Dios, are most impacted by the storm, with hundreds of homes, buildings, and bridges damaged or destroyed.

The escalation in violence in Haiti has displaced over 85,000 people since September, including 40,000 who have been displaced in the past week alone. Non-state armed group activity has also constrained food systems throughout the country, leaving over 2 million people facing IPC4 levels of food insecurity, with nearly 6,000 people facing catastrophic (IPC5) levels of food insecurity. As armed groups have announced their intent to escalate the situation, further displacement and violence is expected in the coming months

Since 23 September, nearly 40,000 people have fled the escalating crisis in Lebanon, through Syria to Iraq. Most  arrivals in Iraq are through the Al-Qaim border crossing from Syria, although others are arriving via air into Baghdad and Najaf. Please note, the impacts of the crisis in Lebanon and Syria are classified separately.

Since late October, at least 550 cholera cases have been reported in South Sudan from 4 countries, Malakal, Renk, and Aweil West, all in the northern part of the country, as well as Juba county towards the south. The outbreak has been exacerbated by recent flooding as well as over-crowded conditions for refugees and returnees in Renk county.
